# Data/Model Consistency Checklist: Orchestrated Spec-Kit Command Workflow Platform

**Purpose**: Validate whether the requirements and design artifacts define a complete, clear, and internally consistent data model for workflow state, artifact lineage, review decisions, and model routing.
**Created**: 2026-03-25
**Feature**: [spec.md](D:/资料/资料/公司/个人/test/ai-agent-playground/spec-kit_project/spec-kit-project/specs/001-multi-agent-specflow/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Are persistence requirements defined for every key entity named in the spec, including whether each one must be stored, derived, or transient? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Data Model §Entities]
- [x] CHK002 - Does the spec define how `OutputBundle` is represented or why it is intentionally omitted from the persisted data model? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities, Data Model §Entities]
- [x] CHK003 - Are requirements complete for how `WorkflowRun`, `CommandExecution`, `StageExecution`, and `ArtifactDocument` link to each other across a full command lifecycle? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Data Model §Relationship Summary]
- [x] CHK004 - Are model-routing requirements specific enough to define what must be stored for primary model choice, fallback choice, and fallback rationale? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013, Data Model §ModelAssignment]
- [x] CHK005 - Are overwrite and version-history requirements complete enough to define both the current artifact record and the preserved prior-version record on every rerun? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015a, Spec §SC-007, Data Model §ArtifactDocument, Data Model §ArtifactVersion]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 - Is the uniqueness scope of `feature_key` clearly defined across workspaces, reruns, and repeated feature names? [Clarity, Data Model §WorkflowRun]
- [x] CHK007 - Is the term "important artifacts" precise enough to determine which outputs must generate `ReviewDecision` records and which may bypass review? [Ambiguity, Spec §Agent Workflow & Review, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK008 - Is "latest user-edited local artifact files" defined with enough precision to determine the source of truth when database metadata and local file timestamps disagree? [Clarity, Spec §FR-019, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK009 - Are the boundaries between `completed`, `blocked`, `failed`, `review_required`, and `incomplete` outcomes clearly defined across run, command, stage, and artifact records? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §State Transitions]
- [x] CHK010 - Is the distinction between "communication records," "handoff payloads," and "execution summaries" explicit enough to avoid overlapping or duplicate storage responsibilities? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-014, Data Model §CommunicationEnvelope]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 - Do the dedicated-agent requirements align with the data model well enough to show one unambiguous ownership path from agent profile to stage execution to command execution? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Agent Workflow & Review, Data Model §StageExecution, Data Model §AgentProfile]
- [x] CHK012 - Do rerun requirements, blocked-review requirements, and artifact supersession rules remain consistent about whether the same command record is retried or a new command execution record is created? [Consistency, Spec §FR-020, Spec §FR-015a, Data Model §CommandExecution, Data Model §ArtifactDocument]
- [x] CHK013 - Are review-blocking requirements consistent between user stories, functional requirements, and state transitions about when a command may be marked complete? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-016, Data Model §CommandExecution]
- [x] CHK014 - Do command-prerequisite requirements align with the modeled `prerequisite_snapshot` field closely enough to define what evidence must be stored for blocked commands? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-005a, Data Model §CommandExecution]
- [x] CHK015 - Are auditability requirements consistent about whether model assignment changes, fallback events, review outcomes, and artifact lineage must all be queryable from the same run history? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §SC-004, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK016 - Can the auditability requirement be objectively verified from the written requirements, including what exact records must exist for an operator to trace an artifact in under one minute? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK017 - Are version-history success criteria measurable enough to determine whether rerun traceability is sufficient without inventing extra implementation-specific checks? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007, Spec §FR-015a]
- [x] CHK018 - Are completion criteria defined for partial-failure cases, such as successful review plus failed local write, in a way that can be objectively judged from stored state? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §State Transitions]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 - Are requirements defined for the initial zero-history state, where a new workflow run has no command executions, no artifacts, and no prior review records yet? [Coverage, Gap, Data Model §WorkflowRun, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK020 - Are recovery requirements specified for the case where a review-blocked command is rerun after manual edits but its previous artifact versions and review records must remain traceable? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-020, Data Model §ArtifactVersion, Data Model §ReviewDecision]
- [x] CHK021 - Are requirements complete for fallback-model scenarios, including what state changes and audit records must be captured when the preferred model is unavailable? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013, Data Model §ModelAssignment]
- [x] CHK022 - Are requirements defined for timeline/history views when some command records are complete, some are blocked, and some local artifact writes have only partially succeeded? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-011]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK023 - Does the spec define what happens to data integrity when local Markdown output succeeds for some artifacts and fails for others within the same command? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK024 - Are requirements defined for preserving or reconciling version metadata when overwrite-on-rerun succeeds but backup creation fails? [Gap, Spec §FR-015a, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK025 - Is conflict handling specified for cases where local files are manually edited after approval but before the next command snapshots its input state? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-019, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 - Are retention, privacy, or storage-growth requirements defined for communication envelopes, review decisions, and artifact versions, or are these intentionally out of scope? [Gap, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-014, Data Model §CommunicationEnvelope]
- [x] CHK027 - Are performance requirements allocated clearly enough to the data model to determine which queries must support command availability, prerequisite validation, and audit lookups within the stated targets? [Non-Functional, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-004, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK028 - Are durability requirements specified for the boundary between MySQL state and filesystem artifact writes so consistency expectations are testable after crashes or retries? [Gap, Research §Decision 3, Research §Decision 5, Plan §Technical Context]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK029 - Are assumptions about one workspace at a time, dozens of runs per day, and hundreds of artifact revisions translated into explicit data-volume or indexing requirements where consistency depends on scale? [Assumption, Plan §Technical Context, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK030 - Are external dependency assumptions documented for Hugging Face model availability and MySQL availability in a way that clarifies which state transitions must still be recorded during outages? [Dependency, Spec §FR-012, Spec §Edge Cases, Research §Decision 7]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK031 - Is it unambiguous whether a blocked command remains the current command of the workflow run until rerun completes, or whether the workflow returns to a neutral state? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 2, Data Model §WorkflowRun, Data Model §CommandExecution]
- [x] CHK032 - Do the requirements clearly separate "history for audit," "history for rollback," and "history for UI display," or could these be interpreted as three different persistence scopes? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-015a, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK033 - Are there any unresolved conflicts between the requirement to keep output paths stable and the requirement to preserve prior versions without creating ambiguous duplicates? [Conflict, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-015a, Spec §SC-007]

## Notes

- Focus areas: data/model consistency, artifact lineage, review-state persistence, rerun traceability, and model-routing auditability
- Depth: strict
- Audience: author self-check
- Use this checklist to challenge the written requirements and design artifacts before implementation expands the schema or persistence layer
- Reviewed on 2026-03-25 against `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `data-model.md`, `research.md`, and `contracts/workflow-api.yaml`; all previously open items were resolved by adding persistence classification, rerun semantics, source-of-truth rules, retention/privacy constraints, durability guarantees, and explicit incomplete-state modeling.
